state.action: Elias stands in the dusty aisle of the basement theatre, hands clasped behind his back. The dais is no longer there. No speeches tonight. "You came back. Good. Did you find out more about the Telescreens?"
1) Explain about the feeds you saw. 2) You're extremely nervous about about this. 3) Don't be a fool - quit before you're arrested.

"This is excellent news. Better than I'd dared hope for. Now we can declare a revoution! We need a structure of course, revolutionary theory suggests a cells of three structure to be among the safest options. Arranged as a pyramid, we start as three, myself, Silas and Wynn. Each of us will recruit three below them and so on. If any of us disappears they can only betray a maximum of six others - three below, two others in his cell and one above." He smiles, "although I'm already prepared for such an eventuality."
There's nothing to worry about, I'm on the run already - the party have wanted to get me for years. Think of me as Goldstein, I'm the embodiment of everything the party wishes to erase.

"You will have a special role at the top of the pyramid - you're the intelligence gatherer. If you supply the information we can make it work for the good of everyone. The three Ministries might be practical to start with - Miniluv is out of the question, for now at least. Three truths concealed. You've opened a crack in the telescreen system - now widen it. We need physical evidence from the Ministries. It is for you now, you're unknown to the state - Silas and I are on the run already. Wynn can help but you must get it."
1) Well, I can get into Minitrue but what about the other Ministries? 2) Not a chance, I'll be unpersonned.
Good, start there then. Wynn will help with the others. She's done some research into this and may have found some cracks in the system.

"Sending anyone else will be like sending lambs to the slaughter, you've got a pass already for Heaven's sake." His eyes narrow at you, "the head of an organisation such as ours must be reliable." "Come back again, when you've got something more. We'll work on expanding the network."
You shake your head, "Sorry, I can't do this". Your nerves have got the better of you and you hastily leave the theatre, head hung in shame.
You shake your head again but feel lighter. You haven't been arrested but you know it was close.
